pr.title: fix encoding and path issues on Windows in tools/decomp.py
pr.author: BR-

summary: Small tooling portability fix for Windows users. The PR updated Python helper scripts so generated text files are written with explicit UTF-8 encoding, made tools/decomp.py invoke the current Python interpreter when commands begin with `python`, improved subprocess failure diagnostics by printing command/stdout/stderr to stderr, and added the missing `pycparser` requirement.

key_files.reason: Adjusted subprocess execution and error reporting in `run_cmd` to handle Python command resolution better on Windows.
Added explicit UTF-8 encoding to generated header, source output, and rendered HTML writes.
Added `pycparser>=2.21`, apparently required by the m2ctx/decomp tooling path.

what_changed: `tools/decomp.py` now checks whether the subprocess command starts with `python`; if so, it passes `sys.executable` as the subprocess `executable`, helping use the active interpreter/virtualenv rather than relying on Windows PATH resolution.
`tools/decomp.py` now prints failed command, stdout, and stderr to `sys.stderr` before exiting, instead of only printing the command and stderr with default stdout behavior.
`tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py` now writes generated include-header content with `encoding="utf-8"`.
`tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py` now writes generated source/context output with `encoding="utf-8"`.
`tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py` now writes rendered HTML output with `encoding="utf-8"`.
smart_moves: Used `sys.executable` instead of hard-coding a Python path, preserving virtualenv behavior and avoiding platform-specific assumptions.
Made text output encoding explicit at each file write site touched by the m2ctx flow, reducing Windows default-codepage failures without changing the generated content format.
Improved failure observability for subprocess calls by emitting both stdout and stderr to stderr when a command fails, making CI/local debugging easier.
Fixed a dependency issue in `requirements.txt` rather than relying on transitive or locally installed packages.
decomp_lessons: Decomp helper scripts should not assume POSIX-like Python command resolution; use `sys.executable` when the intent is to run the current Python environment.
When tools generate source, context, header, or HTML files, specify `encoding="utf-8"` explicitly to avoid Windows locale/codepage surprises.
Tooling fixes that affect matching workflows can be small but important: broken context generation or failed subprocess invocation can block decomp work even when game code is unchanged.
Subprocess wrappers should surface command output on failure; otherwise matcher/decompiler/preprocessor failures are harder to diagnose.
naming_conventions: No new project naming convention is evident from the slice.
Existing helper names retained include `resolve_path`, `run_cmd`, `write_header`, and `try_import`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching tactic was changed.
Indirect matching support improved by making `tools/decomp.py` and `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py` more reliable on Windows for generating and rendering decompilation context.
review_feedback: No review comments or issue comments were present in the provided slice.
One review occurred according to the PR metadata, but no review feedback text was included.
follow_up_queries: Find other Python tooling files that call `Path.write_text` without explicit `encoding="utf-8"`.
Find other subprocess wrappers that invoke `python` instead of `sys.executable`.
Check whether `template_path.read_text()` in `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py` should also specify `encoding="utf-8"`, since this PR only changed writes.
Audit decomp/matching tooling dependencies to ensure all imports are represented in `requirements.txt`.
